alternative_translations: one who has entered the stream
definition: A person who has entered the stream to awakening and irreversibly shifted in view and trajectory.
notes: The project keeps stream-enterer because it is the clearest compact doctrinal label in English. The longer phrase one who has entered the stream can be used where a less technical sentence shape is needed. This term marks the first irreversible noble breakthrough and should be heard as a real doctrinal threshold rather than as a vague label for being interested in the teachings.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose where the sentence benefits from an explicit relative clause
context_rules.rendering: one who has entered the stream

context_rules.context: When contrasted with the ordinary person or used as the first member of the liberation sequence
context_rules.notes: Preserve the precision of the stage label.

example_phrases.notes: Common inflected form in descriptions of stage qualities.

authority_basis.scope: Current house policy is preserved from the existing rule-bearing entry; source-specific provenance still needs refinement.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'stream-enterer' as the default rendering in the entry's governed translation contexts unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Yield to recorded context_rules, source-facing technical use, or other explicitly documented local reasons rather than rotating among alternates ad hoc.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents drift back toward 'convert' or other unrecorded alternates.
